Operation 'scrapeGet' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks critical details about inputs (parameters available), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (automatic handling of proxies/CAPTCHAs). Enhanced version clarifies the operation's full capabilities, authentication requirements, and response structure to improve AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Scrape any web page by providing the target URL and your API key. Recommended timeout: 70 seconds.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web page content via GET request
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes the content of any web page by sending a GET request to the target URL. Handles proxies, browsers, and CAPTCHA challenges automatically. Requires authentication via API key. Accepts parameters for customization including timeout (recommended: 70 seconds), rendering options, and proxy configuration. Returns the scraped page content, HTTP status, and metadata. Maximum request size: 50MB.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks critical details about inputs (parameters available), outputs (what is returned), and side effects (automatic handling of proxies/CAPTCHAs). Enhanced version clarifies the operation's full capabilities, authentication requirements, and response structure to improve AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'scrapePost' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original description is too brief and relies on comparison to GET operation. AI agents need independent clarity about POST-specific capabilities. Enhanced version explains when to use POST (form data, POST-only endpoints), clarifies inputs (request body, headers), outputs (response structure), and maintains consistency with scrapeGet operation documentation.

data.current_description: Same as GET but using POST method. Useful for sending form data to the target URL.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web page content via POST request
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes web page content by sending a POST request to the target URL, enabling form data submission and interaction with endpoints requiring POST method. Handles proxies, browsers, and CAPTCHA challenges automatically. Requires authentication via API key. Accepts parameters for request body, headers, timeout (recommended: 70 seconds), and rendering options. Returns the scraped page content, HTTP status, and metadata. Maximum request size: 50MB.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief and relies on comparison to GET operation. AI agents need independent clarity about POST-specific capabilities. Enhanced version explains when to use POST (form data, POST-only endpoints), clarifies inputs (request body, headers), outputs (response structure), and maintains consistency with scrapeGet operation documentation.
